rxn_class_summary: Enantioselective electrochemical cobalt-catalyzed aryl C-H activation enabling C-C, C-N, and C-O bond-forming annulations of carboxylic amides and phosphinamides coupled to the hydrogen evolution reaction at the cathode.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the cyclometalated Co(III) species to higher-valent Co(IV)/Co(III) intermediates that drive C-N/C-O/C-C bond formation via oxidation-induced reductive elimination; protons reduced at the Pt cathode to give H2 (HER) as the terminal sink, with cobalt acting as the inner-sphere electrocatalyst.

key_intermediates.name: Cyclometalated Co(III) complex C1 (CPA1-ligated, C-H activated benzamide)
Co(III)-CPA1 chiral cobaltacycle (point of enantioinduction via inner-sphere coordination of chiral phosphoric acid)
Higher-valent Co(IV) intermediate generated by anodic oxidation
Migratory-insertion alkyl-Co intermediate after alkene insertion

electron_flow: Co(II) is oxidized in situ and undergoes CMD-type C-H metalation of the benzamide to give a chiral cyclometalated Co(III) species; anodic single-electron oxidation generates a Co(IV) (or Co(III/IV/II) manifold) intermediate that, after alkene/alkyne coordination and migratory insertion, undergoes oxidation-induced reductive elimination to forge the C-C/C-N/C-O bond, while protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.
rate_determining_step: Electron transfer (anodic oxidation of the cyclometalated Co(III) intermediate); C-H cleavage and migratory insertion are not rate-limiting (kH/kD ≈ 1.0; no order in maleimide).

probe_reagents.name: Deuterated benzamide [D]5-1
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD ≈ 1.0 in parallel experiments, indicating C-H cleavage is not rate-determining.

probe_reagents.name: Stoichiometric cyclometalated Co(III) complex C1
probe_reagents.observation: Isolated and fully characterized; required electricity for efficient and selective product formation, supporting its role as on-cycle intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: Variation of maleimide concentration (kinetic order)
probe_reagents.observation: No marked correlation of initial rate with maleimide concentration; migratory insertion deemed fast and not rate-limiting.

probe_reagents.name: Variation of current (product formation rate)
probe_reagents.observation: Direct correlation between current and product formation rate, identifying electron transfer as rate-determining.

probe_reagents.name: Nonlinear effect study (varying ee of CPA1)
probe_reagents.observation: Linear relationship between ee of CPA1 and ee of product 3, ruling out self-association or multiligand catalysts.

probe_reagents.name: No-current control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formation in absence of electricity, confirming electrochemical turnover.

probe_reagents.name: Ag2CO3 as chemical oxidant control
probe_reagents.observation: Very low enantioselectivity obtained, highlighting the unique role of anodic oxidation for enantiocontrol.

notes: Authors invoke a Co(III/IV/II) catalytic manifold supported by HRMS and UV-vis spectroelectrochemistry. Inner-sphere coordination of CPA1 at cobalt is proposed as the origin of enantioinduction, rather than an outer-sphere chiral counteranion mechanism. HER occurs at the Pt (or stainless steel) cathode as the sole stoichiometric byproduct; pivalic acid additive prevents cathode passivation.
